content.text: Let me start by reading the file to understand its current content.

Now let me analyze what needs to be done:

**The migration rule** is about the upstream `RowProps` type from `@openshift-console/dynamic-plugin-sdk` changing from `{ obj: D; rowData: R; activeColumnIDs: Set<string>; }` to `{ obj: D; rowData: R; activeColumnIDs: Set<string>; index: number; onSelect?: OnSelect; }`.

**The incident context** says `typeName: RowProps` — this is the type that triggered the rule.

**However**, looking at the actual file, this is a **locally-defined** `RowProps<T>` type in the forklift-console-plugin project. It is NOT the upstream `RowProps` from `@openshift-console/dynamic-plugin-sdk`. This local type has completely different fields (`resourceFields`, `resourceData`, `resourceIndex`, `namespace`, `isSelected`, `isExpanded`, `toggleSelect`, `length`) — none of which match the upstream type's fields (`obj`, `rowData`, `activeColumnIDs`).

This is a **false positive** — the migration rule matched on the type name `RowProps` but this is a different, project-local type definition that happens to share the same name. It is not using or extending the upstream SDK's `RowProps` type. Making changes here would be incorrect and would break the project's own API.

## Changes Applied

**No changes applied.** This is a false positive match. The `RowProps<T>` type at line 9 is a locally-defined type in the forklift-console-plugin project with entirely different fields (`resourceFields`, `resourceData`, `resourceIndex`, `namespace`, etc.) than the upstream SDK's `RowProps` (`obj`, `rowData`, `activeColumnIDs`). The migration rule matched on the type name only, but this type is not derived from or related to the upstream `@openshift-console/dynamic-plugin-sdk` `RowProps` type. No edit is warranted.
